terms.definition: A range [L, U] computed from sample data by a specified procedure such that, under repeated independent sampling, approximately (1 − α)×100% of intervals from that procedure contain the fixed true parameter.
The target long-run coverage probability (1 − α) of a CI procedure, e.g. 95% means α = 0.05; it is a property of the method, not a post-data probability for one interval.
Half the width of a symmetric CI: critical value × standard error; the amount added and subtracted from the point estimate.
The estimated standard deviation of a sample statistic (e.g. SE(x̄) = s/√n); it measures how much the point estimate would vary across repeated samples.
A quantile from a reference distribution (z_{α/2} or t_{α/2, df}) that sets the CI width for confidence level (1 − α); larger critical values yield wider intervals.
